sections.title: Overview and Motivation
sections.description: Introduces the two-sector response kernel, its physical motivation, and the structure of the derivation.
prerequisites: General familiarity with field-theoretic response functions.

sections.title: Definitions and Notation
sections.description: Defines all symbols: R_ij(k), A_ij(k), B_ij(k), delta_ij, epsilon, Lambda, f(k), g(k), h(k), C_0, C_2, I_ij, F(k), G_ij(k). Establishes conventions for Einstein summation, dimension d = 2 + epsilon, and integration measure dk/k.
prerequisites: Tensor algebra in flat Euclidean space.
outputs_provided: definitions.json artifact with complete symbol table and conventions.

sections.title: Starting Expression
sections.description: Presents the defining integral for the two-point response kernel R_ij as a radial momentum integral of the sum of sector A and sector B kernels, with O(epsilon^2) error estimate.

sections.title: Scientific Identity: Product Rule
sections.description: Introduces the product rule identity partial_k [F(k) G_ij(k)] = F'(k) G_ij(k) + F(k) partial_k G_ij(k) as a tool for reorganizing the integrand. Lists assumptions: differentiability of F and G on (0, Lambda], flat-space index commutation.

sections.title: Reorganization via Product Rule
sections.description: Applies the product rule identity to reorganize the integrand, separating total derivative terms (which may be evaluated by boundary conditions) from remainder terms.
outputs_provided: Reorganized expression, intermediate between starting and sector decomposition.

sections.title: Sector Decomposition
sections.description: Decomposes the response kernel into Sector A (scalar + longitudinal, carrying f(k) and g(k)) and Sector B (transverse, carrying h(k)). Proves exact reconstruction: R_ij = R_ij^(A) + R_ij^(B).
prerequisites: Tensor projection operators: delta_ij, k_i k_j / k^2, and their orthogonal complements.

sections.title: Projection onto Basis Tensors
sections.description: Projects the sector-decomposed expression onto the isotropic (delta_ij) and anisotropic (I_ij) basis tensors, reducing the tensorial structure to scalar coefficient equations.
prerequisites: Tensor contraction identities in d dimensions.
outputs_provided: Projected scalar equations for C_0 and C_2.
sections.omission_justification: Integration by parts was not used or authorized in this derivation.

sections.title: Integration of Sector A
sections.description: Carries out the radial momentum integration for sector A, evaluating boundary contributions at k = 0 and k = Lambda under physically motivated boundary conditions.
outputs_provided: Integrated form of sector A contribution to C_0 and C_2.

sections.title: Integration of Sector B
sections.description: Carries out the radial momentum integration for the transverse sector B, evaluating boundary terms and combining with sector A results.
prerequisites: Radial integration with cutoff regularization. Transverse projector properties.
outputs_provided: Integrated form of sector B contribution to C_0 and C_2.

sections.title: Integrated Result: R_ij = C_0 delta_ij + C_2 I_ij
sections.description: Combines the integrated sector A and sector B results to obtain the final compact form with two independent coefficients C_0 and C_2 multiplying basis tensors delta_ij and I_ij. Error terms are O(epsilon^2, 1/Lambda).
prerequisites: Integration results from SEC_08 and SEC_09.

sections.title: Numerical Verification
sections.description: Provides numerical checks of the integrated result against the starting expression for sample values of f(k), g(k), h(k), epsilon, and Lambda. Confirms convergence within stated error bounds.
prerequisites: Numerical integration methods. The 96-term expression in long_expression_artifact.txt.
outputs_provided: Numerical regression test results confirming R_ij - (C_0 delta_ij + C_2 I_ij) ~ O(epsilon^2, 1/Lambda).

sections.title: Physical Interpretation
sections.description: Interprets the coefficients C_0 (isotropic response) and C_2 (anisotropic response) in terms of observable transport signatures. Discusses the physical meaning of the sector A (compressible) and sector B (incompressible) decomposition.
prerequisites: General knowledge of linear response theory.
outputs_provided: physical_interpretation_mapping.json, six interpretations covering all key equations.

sections.title: Limiting Case: epsilon -> 0
sections.description: Analyzes the dimensional reduction limit epsilon -> 0 (d = 2). Shows that the anisotropic tensor I_ij vanishes identically in d = 2, leaving a purely isotropic response R_ij = C_0^(0) delta_ij.
prerequisites: Results from SEC_10. Properties of symmetric traceless tensors in d = 2.

sections.title: Supplement Request and Reproducibility Package
sections.description: Documents the complete supplement request including all artifacts, their SHAs, reproducibility instructions, and expected verification outcomes.
